욥기 31:1.content: “I made a covenant with my eyes, how then should I look lustfully at a young woman?

욥기 31:2.content: For what is the portion from God above, and the heritage from the Almighty on high?

욥기 31:3.content: Is it not calamity to the unrighteous, and disaster to the workers of iniquity?

욥기 31:4.content: Doesn’t he see my ways, and count all my steps?

욥기 31:5.content: “If I have walked with falsehood, and my foot has hurried to deceit

욥기 31:6.content: (let me be weighed in an even balance, that God may know my integrity);

욥기 31:7.content: if my step has turned out of the way, if my heart walked after my eyes, if any defilement has stuck to my hands,

욥기 31:8.content: then let me sow, and let another eat. Yes, let the produce of my field be rooted out.

욥기 31:9.content: “If my heart has been enticed to a woman, and I have laid wait at my neighbor’s door,

욥기 31:10.content: then let my wife grind for another, and let others sleep with her.

욥기 31:11.content: For that would be a heinous crime. Yes, it would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges;

욥기 31:12.content: for it is a fire that consumes to destruction, and would root out all my increase.

욥기 31:13.content: “If I have despised the cause of my male servant or of my female servant, when they contended with me,

욥기 31:14.content: what then will I do when God rises up? When he visits, what will I answer him?

욥기 31:15.content: Didn’t he who made me in the womb make him? Didn’t one fashion us in the womb?

욥기 31:16.content: “If I have withheld the poor from their desire, or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail,

욥기 31:17.content: or have eaten my morsel alone, and the fatherless has not eaten of it

욥기 31:18.content: (no, from my youth he grew up with me as with a father, I have guided her from my mother’s womb);

욥기 31:19.content: if I have seen any perish for want of clothing, or that the needy had no covering;

욥기 31:20.content: if his heart hasn’t blessed me, if he hasn’t been warmed with my sheep’s fleece;

욥기 31:21.content: if I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless, because I saw my help in the gate,

욥기 31:22.content: then let my shoulder fall from the shoulder blade, and my arm be broken from the bone.

욥기 31:23.content: For calamity from God is a terror to me. Because of his majesty, I can do nothing.

욥기 31:24.content: “If I have made gold my hope, and have said to the fine gold, ‘You are my confidence;’

욥기 31:25.content: If I have rejoiced because my wealth was great, and because my hand had gotten much;

욥기 31:26.content: if I have seen the sun when it shined, or the moon moving in splendor,

욥기 31:27.content: and my heart has been secretly enticed, and my hand threw a kiss from my mouth,

욥기 31:28.content: this also would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges; for I should have denied the God who is above.

욥기 31:29.content: “If I have rejoiced at the destruction of him who hated me, or lifted up myself when evil found him

욥기 31:30.content: (yes, I have not allowed my mouth to sin by asking his life with a curse);

욥기 31:31.content: if the men of my tent have not said, ‘Who can find one who has not been filled with his meat?’

욥기 31:32.content: (the foreigner has not camped in the street, but I have opened my doors to the traveler);

욥기 31:33.content: if like Adam I have covered my transgressions, by hiding my iniquity in my heart,

욥기 31:34.content: because I feared the great multitude, and the contempt of families terrified me, so that I kept silence, and didn’t go out of the door—

욥기 31:35.content: oh that I had one to hear me! Behold, here is my signature! Let the Almighty answer me! Let the accuser write my indictment!

욥기 31:36.content: Surely I would carry it on my shoulder; and I would bind it to me as a crown.

욥기 31:37.content: I would declare to him the number of my steps. as a prince would I go near to him.

욥기 31:38.content: If my land cries out against me, and its furrows weep together;

욥기 31:39.content: if I have eaten its fruits without money, or have caused its owners to lose their life,

욥기 31:40.content: let briers grow instead of wheat, and stinkweed instead of barley.” The words of Job are ended.
